---
title: '7 Insane Ways Music Affects The Body (According to Science) | Cracked.com'
date: '2014-11-04T05:52:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '2874630612058874237'
blogger_filename: '/2014/11/7-insane-ways-music-affects-body.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'White Noise'
  - 'Research'
---

<div dir="ltr"><br />  Melodic intonation therapy, or singing until you can talk, takes advantage of the fact that language functions are located in the left brain, but music lives over on the right side of the brain. So, when that asshole stroke robs you of your ability to speak, you can train your brain to move those functions to the other side by associating music with language. This essentially rewires a lifetime of growth and an entire history of evolution into meaninglessness interpretations of random head noises from a guy who hasn't shaved his beard since the 70s.</div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://www.cracked.com/article_18405_7-insane-ways-music-affects-body-according-to-science.html">http://www.cracked.com/article_18405_7-insane-ways-music-affects-body-according-to-science.html</a></div><div dir="ltr">DarrellWolfe.com via Mobile</div>
